---
title: "CogDyn Lab"
subtitle: "Understanding cognition, dynamics, and intelligent behavior"
listing: false
---

::: {.hero-banner}
# CogDyn Lab

We study how cognitive systems adapt, learn, decide, and interact over time.

[Explore our research](research.qmd){.btn .btn-primary} [Join us](join.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::

## About

The **Cognitive Dynamics Lab** investigates the mechanisms underlying learning, decision-making, attention, memory, and adaptive behavior. We combine computational modeling, behavioral experiments, data analysis, and interdisciplinary collaboration.

Replace this paragraph with the lab's actual mission statement, university affiliation, and major research keywords.

## Research themes

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Computational cognition

Models of learning, inference, decision-making, and cognitive control.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Behavioral dynamics

Experiments and analyses of how behavior evolves across time and context.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Human-AI interaction

Understanding how people collaborate, adapt, and reason with intelligent systems.
:::
:::

## Latest news

- **2026-06-19** — Website launched. Replace this with recent lab updates.
- **2026-06-01** — Add accepted papers, awards, recruitment posts, seminars, and grants here.

[See all news](news.qmd)

## Open positions

We welcome motivated graduate students, undergraduate interns, and collaborators interested in cognitive dynamics, computational modeling, and human behavior. See [Join Us](join.qmd) for details.
